message:
Overhaul README with badges, mermaid diagram, and comprehensive documentation

Transform README.md from a terse technical document into a polished,
user-friendly guide with visual hierarchy, quick-start instructions, and
complete feature coverage.

Visual enhancements:
- Added emoji prefix (🔎) to project title for visual identity
- Added status badges at top: platform support (Linux/macOS/Windows),
  Rust nightly requirement, alpha status
- Introduced emoji section headers (✨ Highlights, 🚀 Quickstart, 🛠️ CLI,
  🧠 Architecture, 🔒 Integrity, ⚙️ Environment, 🧪 Developer, 🔍 Connectors,
  🩺 Troubleshooting, 📜 License, 🤝 Contributing)
- Added mermaid architecture flowchart showing data flow from agent logs through
  connectors → storage (SQLite + FTS5) → search (Tantivy + fallback) → UI
  * Uses pastel color scheme with classDef for visual clarity
  * Shows all six agent sources (Codex, Cline, Gemini, Claude, OpenCode, Amp)
  * Illustrates dual-backend search architecture and TUI/headless UI modes

Content restructuring:
- Moved installation one-liner to prominent position at top (inside centered div)
  for immediate discoverability
- Reorganized from developer-first to user-first flow: Install → Index → Use TUI
- Expanded Quickstart section with step-by-step instructions and flag documentation
- Added dedicated TUI keymap section with all hotkeys and their functions
- Created comprehensive CLI reference section with all commands and flags
- Split troubleshooting into dedicated section with common issues and solutions

New sections added:
- Highlights (✨): bulleted feature overview replacing scattered technical notes
  * Three-pane TUI with filter pills and detail view
  * Connector coverage for all six agents with incremental ingestion
  * Indexing pipeline (SQLite + Tantivy, watch mode, append-only updates)
  * Search capabilities (multi-field, filters, pagination, snippets)
  * Logging/tracing infrastructure
  * Installer features (checksum, easy mode, rustup bootstrap)
  * Test coverage (unit, fixtures, E2E, CI)
- Quickstart (🚀): streamlined 3-step flow with install → index → tui
  * Includes flag reference for installer (--dest, --system, --artifact-url, etc.)
  * Documents RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1 for CI environments
  * Shows headless TUI smoke test command for CI validation
- TUI keymap: comprehensive hotkey reference
  * Search: type to filter, / focuses query input
  * Filters: a/w/f/t to add, A/W/F to clear individual, x to clear all
  * Navigation: j/k or arrows, PgUp/PgDn for pagination
  * Detail tabs: Tab cycles Messages/Snippets/Raw
  * Help/theme: ? toggles help overlay, h toggles light/dark theme
  * Actions: o opens in $EDITOR, q/Esc quits
- CLI reference (🛠️): all subcommands with flags
  * index [--full] [--watch] [--data-dir DIR] [--db PATH]
  * tui [--data-dir DIR] [--once]
  * completions <shell>, man
  * Explains --full (truncates DB/index, re-ingests), --watch (debounced file
    watcher with mtime routing), --data-dir (platform data dir override)
- Architecture (🧠): high-level design overview
  * Model layer: normalized domain types (agents/workspaces/conversations/messages)
  * Storage: rusqlite with WAL, migrations, schema_version, FTS5 mirror, rebuild_fts
  * Search: Tantivy schema (agent/workspace/source_path/msg_idx/created_at/title/
    content), SQLite FTS fallback
  * Connectors: detection + scan with since_ts filtering, external_id dedupe,
    idx resequencing, workspace/source path propagation
  * UI: ratatui layout with filter pills, badges, themed detail pane, status/footer,
    headless once-mode for CI
- Integrity & safety (🔒): installer security features
  * sha256 verification with auto-fetch or explicit checksum
  * Temp workdir + lock file per run
  * No destructive file ops, user-local installation by default
  * rustup nightly bootstrap only when needed (skippable via env)
- Environment (⚙️): configuration notes
  * Loads .env via dotenvy::dotenv().ok()
  * Default data dir from directories::ProjectDirs, override via --data-dir
  * Notes to not overwrite .env
- Developer workflow (🧪): streamlined command reference
  * cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy, cargo test
  * E2E test commands with --test-threads=1 for isolation
  * Replaces verbose "Toolchain & dependency policy" section
- Connectors coverage (🔍): path patterns for all six agents
  * Codex: ~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl
  * Cline: VS Code globalStorage saoudrizwan.claude-dev task dirs
  * Gemini CLI: ~/.gemini/tmp/**
  * Claude Code: ~/.claude/projects/** + .claude/.claude.json
  * OpenCode: .opencode SQLite DBs (project/global)
  * Amp: VS Code globalStorage + ~/.local/share/amp caches
- Troubleshooting (🩺): common issues and solutions
  * Checksum mismatch: ensure .sha256 reachable, check proxies/firewalls
  * Binary not on PATH: append ~/.local/bin to PATH, re-open shell
  * Nightly missing in CI: use RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1 or allow rustup bootstrap
  * Watch mode not triggering: check watch_state.json updates and connector roots
- Contributing (🤝): development guidelines
  * Follow nightly toolchain, run fmt/clippy/test before changes
  * Keep output colorful and informative
  * Avoid destructive commands and regex-based mass scripts

Removed/condensed:
- Merged "Toolchain & dependency policy" into Developer workflow section
- Removed verbose "Structure (scaffold)" section (redundant with Architecture)
- Consolidated "Env loading" into Environment section
- Removed separate "Dev commands" section (now in Developer workflow)
- Condensed "Install" section (moved one-liner to top, details to Quickstart)
- Merged "Usage (TUI & indexing)" into Quickstart and TUI keymap sections
- Streamlined "Connectors & watch coverage" into Connectors coverage

Installation improvements:
- Prominently features one-line curl|bash install with --easy-mode --verify flags
- Shows PowerShell one-liner for Windows (irm | iex pattern)
- Documents all installer flags in Quickstart section
- Notes RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1 for CI environments
- Removed Homebrew reference (not yet published)
- Removed cargo-dist workflow details (internal implementation detail)

Mermaid diagram details:
- Pastel color scheme with five distinct color classes for visual clarity
- Shows linear data flow: Sources → Connectors → Storage → Search → UI
- Storage split into SQLite (WAL, migrations) and FTS5 mirror
- Search shows both Tantivy index and SQLite FTS fallback
- UI shows both TUI (filters + detail + help) and headless once-mode
- Arrows indicate data dependencies (connectors write to storage, storage feeds
  search indexes, search backends feed UI layers)

Why this matters:
- Lowers barrier to entry for new users with immediate install → index → use flow
- Improves discoverability of features (filters, open-in-editor, themes, etc.)
- Provides visual hierarchy with emoji headers for easy scanning
- Documents all CLI flags and environment variables in one place
- Includes troubleshooting section to reduce support burden
- Professional presentation for potential users evaluating the tool

The README now serves as both a quick-start guide and comprehensive reference,
with visual polish that matches the colorful TUI experience.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,60 +1,167 @@ -# coding-agent-search +# 🔎 coding-agent-search -Unified TUI for local coding-agent history search (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cline, OpenCode, Amp). +![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-Linux%20%7C%20macOS%20%7C%20Windows-blue.svg) +![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-nightly-orange.svg) +![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-alpha-purple.svg) -## Toolchain & dependency policy -- Toolchain: pinned to latest Rust nightly via `rust-toolchain.toml` (rustfmt, clippy included). -- Crates: track latest releases with wildcard constraints (`*`). Run `cargo update` regularly to pick up fixes. -- Edition: 2024. +Unified TUI to search and browse local coding-agent history (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cline, OpenCode, Amp) with fast indexing, filters, and a colorful terminal experience. -## Env loading -Load `.env` at startup using dotenvy (see `src/main.rs`); do not use `std::env::var` without calling `dotenvy::dotenv().ok()` first. +
-## Dev commands (nightly) +```bash +# Fast path: checksum-verified install + self-test +curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.sh \ + | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify +``` + +```powershell +# Windows (PowerShell) +irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.ps1 | iex +install.ps1 -EasyMode -Verify +``` + +
+ +--- + +## ✨ Highlights +- **Three-pane TUI** (ratatui): live search, filter pills, rich detail view, open-in-editor, help overlay, light/dark themes. +- **Connectors** for Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cline (VS Code), OpenCode, Amp; incremental since_ts ingestion; source paths preserved. +- **Indexing pipeline**: normalized SQLite + Tantivy; FTS5 mirror; append-only updates; watch-mode with mtime routing and watch_state persistence. +- **Search**: multi-field (title/content) with agent/workspace/time filters, pagination, snippets, and Tantivy fallback to SQLite when needed. +- **Logging & tracing**: spans for connectors/indexer/search to aid debugging and tests. +- **Installer**: curl|bash or pwsh with checksum enforcement, optional artifact override, easy/normal modes, rustup nightly bootstrap, PATH hints, self-test and quickstart hooks. +- **Tests & CI**: unit, connector fixtures, storage/indexer/search/TUI snapshots, installer e2e (file:// artifacts), headless TUI smoke; CI runs fmt/clippy/check/test + e2e. + +## 🚀 Quickstart +1) **Install** (easy-mode shown): + ```bash + curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.sh \ + | bash -s -- --easy-mode --verify + ``` + - Flags: `--dest DIR`, `--system`, `--artifact-url`, `--checksum`, `--checksum-url`, `--quickstart`, `--quiet`. + - Skipping rustup: set `RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1` (for environments with nightly already installed). +2) **Index** your logs: + ```bash + coding-agent-search index --full + # optional: --data-dir /path/to/state + ``` +3) **Launch TUI**: + ```bash + coding-agent-search tui + # headless smoke: TUI_HEADLESS=1 coding-agent-search tui --once --data-dir + ``` + +### TUI keymap (defaults) +- Search: type; `/` focuses query. +- Filters: `a` agent, `w` workspace, `f` from, `t` to; `A/W/F` clear, `x` clear all. +- Navigation: `j/k` or arrows, `PgUp/PgDn` paginate. +- Detail tabs: `Tab` cycles Messages/Snippets/Raw. +- Help/theme: `?` toggle help, `h` toggle theme. +- Actions: `o` open hit in `$EDITOR`, `q`/`Esc` quit. + +## 🛠️ CLI reference +```bash +coding-agent-search index [--full] [--watch] [--data-dir DIR] [--db PATH] +coding-agent-search tui [--data-dir DIR] [--once] +coding-agent-search completions +coding-agent-search man +``` +- **index --full** truncates DB/index (non-destructive to source logs) then re-ingests. +- **index --watch** debounced file watcher; routes changes to matching connector; maintains `watch_state.json`. +- **Data locations**: defaults to platform data dir (`directories`); override with `--data-dir`. + +## 🧠 Architecture +- **Model layer**: normalized agents/workspaces/conversations/messages/snippets (`src/model`). +- **Storage**: rusqlite with WAL pragmas, migrations, schema_version, FTS5 mirror; append-only insert/update; rebuild_fts helper. +- **Search**: Tantivy schema (agent, workspace, source_path, msg_idx, created_at, title, content); SQLite FTS fallback. +- **Connectors**: detection + scan with since_ts filtering, external_id dedupe, idx resequencing, workspace/source path propagation. +- **UI**: ratatui layout with filter pills, badges, themed detail pane, status/footer; headless once-mode for CI. + +```mermaid +flowchart LR + classDef pastel fill:#f4f2ff,stroke:#c2b5ff,color:#2e2963; + classDef pastel2 fill:#e6f7ff,stroke:#9bd5f5,color:#0f3a4d; + classDef pastel3 fill:#e8fff3,stroke:#9fe3c5,color:#0f3d28; + classDef pastel4 fill:#fff7e6,stroke:#f2c27f,color:#4d350f; + classDef pastel5 fill:#ffeef2,stroke:#f5b0c2,color:#4d1f2c; + + subgraph Sources + A[Codex + Cline + Gemini + Claude + OpenCode + Amp]:::pastel + end + + subgraph Connectors + C1[Detect & scan
since_ts filtering
external_id dedupe]:::pastel2 + end + + subgraph Storage + S1[SQLite (WAL) + schema_version + migrations]:::pastel3 + S2[FTS5 mirror]:::pastel3 + end + + subgraph Search + T1[Tantivy index
agent/workspace/content]:::pastel4 + F1[SQLite FTS fallback]:::pastel4 + end + + subgraph UI + U1[TUI (ratatui) + filters + detail + help]:::pastel5 + U2[Headless once-mode]:::pastel5 + end + + A --> C1 --> S1 + C1 --> S2 + S1 --> T1 + S2 --> F1 + T1 --> U1 + F1 --> U1 + T1 --> U2 + F1 --> U2 +``` + +## 🔒 Integrity & safety +- Installer requires sha256 verification (auto-fetches `.sha256` or uses provided CHECKSUM). +- Temporary workdir + lock per run; no destructive file ops; installs to user-local by default. +- rustup nightly bootstrap only when cargo/nightly missing (skippable via env). + +## ⚙️ Environment +- Loads `.env` via `dotenvy::dotenv().ok()`; configure API/base paths there (see pattern in code). Do not overwrite `.env`. +- Default data dir: `directories::ProjectDirs` for `coding-agent-search`; override via flags. + +## 🧪 Developer workflow +- `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo check --all-targets` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` -- `cargo fmt --check` -- `cargo test` (unit + snapshots) -- `cargo test --test install_scripts -- --test-threads=1` (file:// checksum pass/fail for install.sh/install.ps1) -- `cargo test --test e2e_index_tui -- --test-threads=1` (index --full then `tui --once` headless) - -Env knobs: -- `TUI_HEADLESS=1` with `coding-agent-search tui --once` skips terminal setup for CI. -- `--data-dir` flag on `index` and `tui` allows isolated test dirs; `XDG_DATA_HOME` also respected. - -## Install -- Shell (Linux/macOS): `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.sh | sh` - Flags: `--version vX.Y.Z`, `--dest DIR`, `--easy-mode`, `OWNER/REPO override`, `--checksum` or `--checksum-url`. The installer *requires* verification: by default it fetches `.sha256` next to the artifact; override with `--checksum` if you already have it. -- PowerShell (Windows): `irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.ps1 | iex` with the same checksum rules (defaults to `.sha256`). -- Homebrew: `brew install coding-agent-search` (formula refuses to install while `sha256` is a placeholder—set real SHA before publishing). -- Releases: built via cargo-dist (`.github/workflows/dist.yml`). The dist workflow now writes `.sha256` files for every artifact and uploads them as workflow artifacts—copy those SHA values into `packaging/homebrew/coding-agent-search.rb` and public release assets before cutting a tag. - -## Usage (TUI & indexing) -- Quickstart: `coding-agent-search index --full` (first run) then `coding-agent-search tui`. -- Headless smoke: `TUI_HEADLESS=1 coding-agent-search tui --once --data-dir ` (used in CI). -- Toggle detailed hotkey legend with `?` (initially shown). Open selected hit in your editor with `o` (uses `$EDITOR` + `$EDITOR_LINE_FLAG`, defaults `vi` and `+` for line jumps). -- Filter hotkeys: `a/w/f/t` to add agent/workspace/time filters, uppercase `A/W/F` to clear each, `x` to clear all; filter pills show their clear keys. -- Indexing full rebuild: `coding-agent-search index --full` truncates SQLite tables and Tantivy, then re-ingests—never deletes source logs. -- Incremental watch: `coding-agent-search index --watch` registers filesystem watchers on all known connector roots. Changes are routed to the relevant connector only, using per-connector mtime high-water marks (`since_ts`) to avoid full rescans. -- Completions/man: `coding-agent-search completions `, `coding-agent-search man`. - -## Structure (scaffold) -- `src/main.rs` – entrypoint wiring tracing + dotenvy -- `src/lib.rs` – library entry -- `src/config/` – configuration layer -- `src/storage/` – SQLite backend -- `src/search/` – Tantivy/FTS -- `src/connectors/` – agent log parsers -- `src/indexer/` – indexing orchestration -- `src/ui/` – Ratatui interface -- `src/model/` – domain types - -## Connectors & watch coverage -- Codex CLI: `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` -- Cline: VS Code globalStorage `saoudrizwan.claude-dev` task dirs -- Gemini CLI: `~/.gemini/tmp/**` -- Claude Code: `~/.claude/projects/**` + `.claude` / `.claude.json` -- OpenCode: `.opencode` SQLite DBs (project/local/global) -- Amp: `sourcegraph.amp` globalStorage + `~/.local/share/amp` JSON caches - -Watch mode listens on these roots and triggers connector-specific scans; `--full` truncates DB/index before ingest (non-destructive to log files). +- `cargo test` +- `cargo test --test install_scripts -- --test-threads=1` +- `cargo test --test e2e_index_tui -- --test-threads=1` +- `cargo test --test e2e_install_easy -- --test-threads=1` + +## 🔍 Connectors coverage +- **Codex**: `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` +- **Cline**: VS Code globalStorage `saoudrizwan.claude-dev` task dirs +- **Gemini CLI**: `~/.gemini/tmp/**` +- **Claude Code**: `~/.claude/projects/**` + `.claude/.claude.json` +- **OpenCode**: `.opencode` SQLite DBs (project/global) +- **Amp**: VS Code globalStorage + `~/.local/share/amp` caches + +## 🩺 Troubleshooting +- **Checksum mismatch**: ensure `.sha256` reachable or pass `--checksum` explicitly; check proxies/firewalls. +- **Binary not on PATH**: append `~/.local/bin` (or your `--dest`) to PATH; re-open shell. +- **Nightly missing in CI**: set `RUSTUP_INIT_SKIP=1` if toolchain is preinstalled; otherwise allow installer to run rustup. +- **Watch mode not triggering**: confirm watch_state.json updates and that connector roots are accessible; mtime-based routing expects real file touch. + +## 📜 License +Project license is recorded in the repository (see LICENSE file). + +## 🤝 Contributing +- Follow nightly toolchain policy and run fmt/clippy/test before sending changes. +- Keep console output colorful and informative; avoid destructive commands; do not use regex-based mass scripts in this repo.
